system_summary: Android systemless rooting and module management framework
initial_notes_thread_model_workload: CPU: boot image patching, script/module execution,system modification logic, IO: filesystem operations, mounting partitions, interacting with Android system interfaces -> hybrid. Staged architecture (install -> patch -> mount etc) -> suggests pipeline. Dedicated worker threads can be used to isolate system-level operations from UI/CLI interactions.

IO: keyboard input, terminal I/O, CPU: glyph rasterization, command submission, frame updates -> hybrid. Event-loop: for input/event dispatch and OS integration, Dedicated worker threads: isolate rendering and heavy computation from event handling

Filesystem traversal and metadata access are I/O-heavy, recursive directory scanning, regex matching, and filtering are CPU/heavy. Rust search tools commonly use rayon-style thread pools for scalable parallel traversal
